ogTitle: Lower Macdonald vs Lower Mangrove: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lower Macdonald and Lower Mangrove.
heading: Lower Macdonald vs Lower Mangrove — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lower Macdonald, New South Wales and Lower Mangrove, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lower Macdonald has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Lower Mangrove (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lower Macdonald or Lower Mangrove safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lower Macdonald has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Lower Mangrove scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lower Macdonald scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Lower Mangrove scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lower Macdonald or Lower Mangrove?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lower Macdonald has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
